meta.title: Mobile Testing
meta.description: Mobile testing with Detox, XCTest, Espresso, Appium, device testing, and accessibility

items.text: Are tests run on all supported OS versions?

baseContent.whatItMeans: Test on the minimum and latest supported iOS and Android versions. API behavior differs across versions.
baseContent.whyItMatters: Code that works on iOS 18 may crash on iOS 16. Android API differences across versions cause runtime crashes.
baseContent.howToVerify: Check CI configuration for OS version matrix. Verify the minimum supported version is included in the test matrix.
baseContent.exampleComment: Tests only run on iOS 18. The app supports iOS 16+. Add iOS 16 to the test matrix to catch compatibility issues.
baseContent.keyTakeaway: Test on minimum and latest OS versions. API differences cause version-specific crashes.

items.text: Are tests run on multiple screen sizes (phone, tablet)?

baseContent.whatItMeans: Test on small phones (iPhone SE), large phones (iPhone Pro Max), and tablets (iPad). Layout breaks on extreme sizes.
baseContent.whyItMatters: A layout that looks perfect on iPhone 15 may overflow on iPhone SE or leave huge gaps on iPad.
baseContent.howToVerify: Check for screen size variety in the test matrix. Verify the smallest and largest supported screens are tested.
baseContent.exampleComment: Tests only run on iPhone 15 simulator. Add iPhone SE (smallest) and iPad (largest) to catch layout issues.
baseContent.keyTakeaway: Test on smallest and largest supported screens. Layout bugs hide at extreme sizes.

items.text: Are critical flows tested on real devices (not just simulators)?

baseContent.whatItMeans: Simulators don't replicate real device behavior: camera, GPS, push notifications, biometrics, and performance constraints.
baseContent.whyItMatters: Simulator tests miss real-device issues: slow GPS lock, camera permission prompts, push notification handling, memory limits.
baseContent.howToVerify: Check if any tests run on real devices or device farms (BrowserStack, Firebase Test Lab, AWS Device Farm).
baseContent.exampleComment: All tests run on simulators. Camera and push notification features are untested. Add real device tests for these flows.
baseContent.keyTakeaway: Use real devices for hardware features (camera, GPS, push). Simulators can't replicate them.

items.text: Are landscape and portrait orientations tested?

baseContent.whatItMeans: Test the app in both portrait and landscape orientations. Verify layout adapts and content remains accessible.
baseContent.whyItMatters: Many apps break in landscape: text gets cut off, buttons become unreachable, or the layout collapses entirely.
baseContent.howToVerify: Check for orientation change tests. Verify key screens render correctly in both orientations.
baseContent.exampleComment: No landscape orientation tests. The form fields overflow in landscape mode. Add orientation change tests for key screens.
baseContent.keyTakeaway: Test both orientations. If landscape isn't supported, verify rotation is properly locked.

items.text: Are light mode and dark mode both tested?

baseContent.whatItMeans: Test the app in both light and dark mode. Verify all text is readable, images have appropriate backgrounds, and colors adapt.
baseContent.whyItMatters: Dark mode with hardcoded black text on dark backgrounds makes content invisible. Users on dark mode see a broken app.
baseContent.howToVerify: Run visual tests in both light and dark mode. Check for hardcoded colors that don't adapt.
baseContent.exampleComment: Dark mode shows black text on a dark gray background — unreadable. Use semantic colors that adapt to the appearance mode.
baseContent.keyTakeaway: Test both appearance modes. Use semantic/dynamic colors that adapt automatically.

items.text: Are touch gestures tested (swipe, pinch, long press, drag)?

baseContent.whatItMeans: Test gesture interactions: swipe to delete, pinch to zoom, long press for context menu, drag to reorder.
baseContent.whyItMatters: Gesture handling bugs cause frustrating UX: swipe doesn't register, pinch zooms the wrong element, drag drops in wrong position.
baseContent.howToVerify: Check for gesture tests in E2E suite. Verify swipe, pinch, long press, and drag gestures are covered.
baseContent.exampleComment: Swipe-to-delete is implemented but has no test. Add a Detox test that swipes a list item and verifies deletion.
baseContent.keyTakeaway: Test all custom gestures. Gestures are hard to debug without automated tests.

items.text: Is navigation tested (back button, deep links, tab switching)?

baseContent.whatItMeans: Test the full navigation stack: forward navigation, back button behavior, tab switching, and deep link handling.
baseContent.whyItMatters: Navigation bugs are the most common mobile crash cause. Back button showing wrong screen or deep links landing on blank screens.
baseContent.howToVerify: Check for navigation tests covering: forward, back, tab switch, deep link entry, and background-to-foreground.
baseContent.exampleComment: No test for back button after deep link entry. Users who open a notification deep link can't navigate back properly.
baseContent.keyTakeaway: Test the full navigation stack. Back button, deep links, and tab switching are common bug sources.

items.text: Are deep links and universal links tested?

baseContent.whatItMeans: Test that deep links open the correct screen with correct data. Test both cold start (app not running) and warm start (app in background).
baseContent.whyItMatters: Broken deep links from notifications, emails, or shared links frustrate users and break marketing campaigns.
baseContent.howToVerify: Test deep links in both cold and warm start. Verify the correct screen opens with correct data. Test invalid deep links.
baseContent.exampleComment: Deep link /order/123 works when app is open but shows a blank screen on cold start. Handle deep links in the app launch flow.
baseContent.keyTakeaway: Test deep links in cold and warm start. Handle invalid and expired deep links gracefully.

items.text: Is keyboard interaction tested (show, hide, scroll, submit)?

baseContent.whatItMeans: Test that the keyboard doesn't obscure input fields, forms scroll when keyboard appears, and submit works via keyboard.
baseContent.whyItMatters: Keyboard covering the submit button is a classic mobile bug. Users can't complete forms if they can't see the button.
baseContent.howToVerify: Test form interactions with the keyboard visible. Verify scrolling, field focusing, and form submission work correctly.
baseContent.exampleComment: The submit button is hidden behind the keyboard on small screens. Add KeyboardAvoidingView and test the interaction.
baseContent.keyTakeaway: Test forms with the keyboard visible. The submit button must always be reachable.

items.text: Are refresh mechanisms tested (pull-to-refresh, retry on error)?

baseContent.whatItMeans: Test pull-to-refresh functionality, retry buttons on error states, and automatic refresh after background-to-foreground.
baseContent.whyItMatters: Broken refresh means users see stale data. If retry doesn't work after a network error, users are stuck.
baseContent.howToVerify: Check for pull-to-refresh tests. Verify data refreshes correctly and loading states are shown during refresh.
baseContent.exampleComment: Pull-to-refresh on the order list doesn't update data from the server. Test that the API is called and new data appears.
baseContent.keyTakeaway: Test refresh mechanisms. Users rely on pull-to-refresh to see updated data.

items.text: Is offline behavior tested (no network, slow network, network recovery)?

baseContent.whatItMeans: Test the app with no network, slow network (3G), and network recovery. Verify cached data is shown and actions queue for later.
baseContent.whyItMatters: Mobile users frequently lose connectivity: tunnels, elevators, airplane mode. The app must handle this gracefully.
baseContent.howToVerify: Test with airplane mode, simulated slow network, and network disconnection mid-request. Verify error UI and recovery.
baseContent.exampleComment: The app shows a blank screen when offline. Display cached data with an offline banner and queue writes for sync.
baseContent.keyTakeaway: Test offline thoroughly. Show cached data, queue actions, and recover gracefully when network returns.

items.text: Is app lifecycle tested (background, foreground, kill, restart)?

baseContent.whatItMeans: Test app behavior when backgrounded, foregrounded, killed by OS, and restarted. Verify state is preserved correctly.
baseContent.whyItMatters: Mobile OSes kill background apps to save memory. If state isn't persisted, users lose in-progress work.
baseContent.howToVerify: Test: fill a form, background the app, foreground it. Verify form data is preserved. Test with app kill and restart.
baseContent.exampleComment: Users filling out a long form lose all data when the app is backgrounded. Persist form state to survive background kills.
baseContent.keyTakeaway: Test background/foreground transitions. Persist important state to survive OS kills.

items.text: Are permission flows tested (grant, deny, revoke)?

baseContent.whatItMeans: Test what happens when users grant, deny, or later revoke permissions (camera, location, notifications, photos).
baseContent.whyItMatters: Crashing when a permission is denied is a common rejection reason in app store review. Handle all permission states.
baseContent.howToVerify: Test with: permission granted, permission denied, permission denied permanently, and permission revoked after granting.
baseContent.exampleComment: The app crashes when camera permission is denied. Show a helpful message explaining why the camera is needed.
baseContent.keyTakeaway: Handle all permission states gracefully. Never crash on denied permissions.

items.text: Is the app tested under memory pressure?

baseContent.whatItMeans: Test app behavior when the device is low on memory. Verify the app doesn't crash and recovers from memory warnings.
baseContent.whyItMatters: Low-memory conditions on older devices cause crashes. The OS sends memory warnings before killing the app.
baseContent.howToVerify: Simulate memory warnings in the simulator. Test with many apps open. Profile memory usage of key flows.
baseContent.exampleComment: The app uses 500MB for image caching without memory warning handling. Implement didReceiveMemoryWarning to clear caches.
baseContent.keyTakeaway: Handle memory warnings. Clear caches and non-essential data to survive low-memory conditions.

items.text: Are interruptions tested (phone call, notification, screenshot)?

baseContent.whatItMeans: Test app behavior during interruptions: incoming call, notification banner, screenshot, screen recording, split screen.
baseContent.whyItMatters: An incoming call during a payment can leave the transaction in an inconsistent state. Interruptions are common.
baseContent.howToVerify: Test critical flows with interruptions. Verify the app resumes correctly after the interruption ends.
baseContent.exampleComment: A phone call during checkout leaves the payment in limbo. Implement proper state management to resume after interruptions.
baseContent.keyTakeaway: Test critical flows with interruptions. The app must resume gracefully.

items.text: Is the app usable with VoiceOver/TalkBack?

baseContent.whatItMeans: All interactive elements must have accessibility labels. Navigation with screen readers must reach all content.
baseContent.whyItMatters: Screen reader users can't use unlabeled buttons or images. This is both an accessibility requirement and often a legal one.
baseContent.howToVerify: Enable VoiceOver (iOS) or TalkBack (Android). Navigate through the app. Verify all elements are announced correctly.
baseContent.exampleComment: The submit button announces 'button' instead of 'Submit Order, button'. Add accessibilityLabel='Submit Order'.
baseContent.keyTakeaway: Test with VoiceOver/TalkBack. Every interactive element needs a meaningful accessibility label.

items.text: Does the app support Dynamic Type / font scaling?

baseContent.whatItMeans: Test with the largest font size setting. Verify text scales, doesn't truncate, and the layout adapts without breaking.
baseContent.whyItMatters: Users with low vision rely on large text. If the app doesn't scale text, it's unusable for these users.
baseContent.howToVerify: Set the device to maximum text size. Navigate through the app. Check for truncation, overlapping, and broken layouts.
baseContent.exampleComment: At maximum Dynamic Type, button text overflows and is cut off. Use flexible layouts that accommodate large text.
baseContent.keyTakeaway: Test at maximum text size. Layouts must adapt to large Dynamic Type settings.

items.text: Are touch targets large enough (44x44pt minimum)?

baseContent.whatItMeans: All tappable elements must have a minimum touch target of 44x44 points (Apple HIG) or 48x48dp (Material Design).
baseContent.whyItMatters: Small touch targets cause frustrating misclicks. Users with motor impairments can't use targets smaller than 44pt.
baseContent.howToVerify: Inspect touch target sizes in the layout inspector. Pay attention to icon buttons, close buttons, and links.
baseContent.exampleComment: This 24x24pt close button is too small. Add padding to make the touch target 44x44pt while keeping the visual size.
baseContent.keyTakeaway: Minimum 44x44pt touch targets. Use padding to increase hit area without changing visual size.

items.text: Do colors meet accessibility contrast requirements?

baseContent.whatItMeans: Text must have 4.5:1 contrast ratio against its background (WCAG AA). Don't use color as the only indicator of state.
baseContent.whyItMatters: Low contrast text is unreadable for users with low vision and in outdoor conditions. Color-blind users miss color-only signals.
baseContent.howToVerify: Use accessibility audit tools. Check text on colored backgrounds. Verify error states use more than just red color.
baseContent.exampleComment: Error messages are indicated only by red text color. Color-blind users miss them. Add an error icon alongside the color.
baseContent.keyTakeaway: Meet contrast requirements. Don't rely on color alone to convey information.

items.text: Are animations reduced when 'Reduce Motion' is enabled?

baseContent.whatItMeans: When the user enables 'Reduce Motion' in system settings, animations should be disabled or simplified to cross-fades.
baseContent.whyItMatters: Animations cause motion sickness for users with vestibular disorders. This is an OS-level accessibility setting.
baseContent.howToVerify: Enable 'Reduce Motion' in device settings. Verify animations are disabled or simplified throughout the app.
baseContent.exampleComment: The page transition animation plays even with Reduce Motion enabled. Check UIAccessibility.isReduceMotionEnabled.
baseContent.keyTakeaway: Respect the Reduce Motion setting. Disable or simplify animations when enabled.

items.text: Are mobile tests running reliably in CI (not flaky)?

baseContent.whatItMeans: Mobile E2E tests in CI should have >95% pass rate. Simulators/emulators need proper startup, timeout, and cleanup.
baseContent.whyItMatters: Flaky mobile tests in CI waste developer time. Every re-run costs 10-30 minutes of CI compute.
baseContent.howToVerify: Check CI pass rates for mobile tests. Identify flaky tests and fix root causes (timing, simulator state).
baseContent.exampleComment: Mobile E2E tests fail 20% of the time in CI due to simulator boot timing. Add a proper health check before test execution.
baseContent.keyTakeaway: Stabilize CI mobile tests. Add simulator health checks and proper wait conditions.

items.text: Are screenshot/snapshot tests used for UI consistency?

baseContent.whatItMeans: Use screenshot testing to catch visual regressions: pixel-level comparison of screens across OS versions and sizes.
baseContent.whyItMatters: Functional tests don't catch visual issues: misaligned elements, wrong fonts, broken layouts, theme inconsistencies.
baseContent.howToVerify: Check for screenshot test configuration. Verify critical screens have baseline screenshots.
baseContent.exampleComment: No screenshot tests for the checkout flow. Add snapshot tests for each step to catch visual regressions.
baseContent.keyTakeaway: Add screenshot tests for critical screens. Catch visual regressions that functional tests miss.

items.text: Is there a proper test build configuration (not using production)?

baseContent.whatItMeans: Tests should run against a test build configuration with test API endpoints, disabled analytics, and test feature flags.
baseContent.whyItMatters: Running tests against production can corrupt real data, send real notifications, and trigger real payments.
baseContent.howToVerify: Check that the test scheme/flavor uses test API URLs. Verify analytics and third-party services are disabled in tests.
baseContent.exampleComment: E2E tests run against the production API. An accidental test order charged a real credit card. Use a test environment.
baseContent.keyTakeaway: Use a dedicated test build configuration. Never run tests against production.

items.text: Are failure artifacts captured (screenshots, logs, videos)?

baseContent.whatItMeans: Capture screenshots, device logs, and video recordings when mobile tests fail. Upload as CI artifacts for debugging.
baseContent.whyItMatters: Mobile test failures are hard to reproduce locally. Screenshots and videos from CI show exactly what went wrong.
baseContent.howToVerify: Check CI configuration for artifact capture on failure. Verify screenshots and logs are uploaded.
baseContent.exampleComment: Mobile test failures have no artifacts. Enable Detox/XCTest screenshot capture and upload device logs as CI artifacts.
baseContent.keyTakeaway: Capture screenshots, videos, and logs on failure. Upload as CI artifacts.

items.text: Are mobile tests optimized for speed (parallel, selective)?

baseContent.whatItMeans: Optimize mobile test execution: parallel test execution, selective test runs on PRs, full suite nightly.
baseContent.whyItMatters: Mobile E2E tests are slow (30+ minutes). Running all tests on every PR blocks developer productivity.
baseContent.howToVerify: Check test execution time. Verify parallel execution is configured. Check for selective test runs on PR changes.
baseContent.exampleComment: Full mobile test suite takes 45 minutes on every PR. Run smoke tests on PR (10 min), full suite nightly.
baseContent.keyTakeaway: Parallelize mobile tests. Run smoke tests on PR, full suite nightly.
